things.name: Lake Tanganyika Beach
things.description: Bujumbura sits on the world's second deepest lake - swim in crystal-clear freshwater

things.name: Living Museum (Musee Vivant)
things.description: Open-air museum with traditional Burundian architecture, crafts and a crocodile pit

things.name: Drum Sanctuary (Gishora)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Burundian royal drum tradition - unforgettable drumming performances

things.name: Source du Nil Monument (Rutovu)
things.description: A boulder marks the furthest source of the River Nile - 80km south of Bujumbura

things.name: Rusizi National Park
things.description: Hippos and crocodiles on the Rusizi River delta flowing into Lake Tanganyika

things.name: Kibira National Park
things.description: Mountain rainforest with chimpanzees in Burundi's largest forest reserve

things.name: Saga Beach
things.description: Popular swimming beach on Lake Tanganyika with fish restaurants

things.name: Bujumbura Central Market
things.description: Vibrant city market with Burundian baskets - among the finest woven baskets in Africa

things.name: Regina Mundi Cathedral
things.description: Belgian colonial cathedral that survived Burundi's turbulent history

things.name: Boko (Brochettes)
things.description: Burundian street food - grilled goat brochettes with plantain and chilli sauce
